verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is secreted by the epididymal epithelium into the epididymal lumen, where it binds to the dorsal region of the sperm head during sperm maturation and remains stably associated with the sperm plasma membrane after washing — a wash-resistant, non-covalent post-translational attachment acquired during transit through a specialised secretory compartment (the epididymis). The sperm-bound pool participates in sperm-egg plasma membrane fusion, indicating outer-leaflet display on a narrow germline lineage. This fits stable_surface_attachment over secreted_only: the recruiter is the sperm surface, but the attachment is not in equilibrium with the soluble pool and persists through capacitation.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether epididymal CRISP1 binding to sperm is sufficiently wash-resistant to qualify as stable_surface_attachment vs tissue_restricted_surface display on the gamete.
